CheckTitle: Ensure Temporary Access Pass maximum lifetime is not excessive
Description: Checks whether Temporary Access Pass (TAP) is enabled with a maximum lifetime exceeding 8 hours (480 minutes). Long-lived TAPs increase the window of opportunity for misuse if intercepted.
Risk: Temporary Access Passes with excessive lifetimes remain valid for long periods, increasing the risk of credential theft and replay attacks. A stolen TAP could be used to register new MFA methods and establish persistent access.

Remediation.Recommendation: Set the Temporary Access Pass maximum lifetime to 480 minutes (8 hours) or less. Prefer one-time use TAPs when possible. Review TAP usage logs regularly.

SourceNotes: TAP lifetime should be minimized to reduce the attack window.
